title: eclipta adpressa
intro: Eclipta adpressa, also known as Eclipta prostrata or False Daisy, is a perennial plant native to Asia and North America, characterized by its small white flowers and hairy stems. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing hair loss, improving liver function, and supporting skin health. Eclipta adpressa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular remedy in folk medicine.
